revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=櫛玉比賣命神社 |画像 = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|2.08|N|132|47|56.13|E}}}} |所在地=愛媛県松山市高田甲702-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|58|2.08|N|132|47|56.13|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神=天道日女命・御炊屋姫命 |社格=式内小社、郷社 |地図 = }}
'''櫛玉比賣命神社'''(くしたまひめのみことじんじゃ)は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]高田に鎮座する[[式内社]]。旧社格は、[[郷社]]。
[[拝殿]]の前方の小山は、[[前方後円墳]]の一部分。
== 概要 == [[応神天皇]]の代に[[勅令]]を奉じ、物部阿佐理命が早風の[[国造]]となり、[[天道日女命]]及び、[[御炊屋姫命]]を奉斎した。
祭神は、天道日女命・御炊屋姫命。
== 境内社 == 素戔嗚神社(祭神:[[素戔嗚命]])
== 例祭 == [[体育の日]]の前々日に行われる。「宵の明星」と呼ばれる。

{{神社 |名称=櫛玉比賣命神社 |画像 = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|2.08|N|132|47|56.13|E}}}} |所在地=愛媛県松山市高田甲702-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|58|2.08|N|132|47|56.13|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神=天道日女命・御炊屋姫命 |社格=式内小社、郷社 |地図 = }}

[[拝殿]]の前方の小山は、[[前方後円墳]]の一部分で、全長が75mの櫛玉比賣命神社古墳と呼ばれる。
== 概要 == [[応神天皇]]の代に[[勅令]]を奉じ、物部阿佐理命が早風の[[国造]]となり、[[天道日女命]]及び、[[御炊屋姫命]]を奉斎した。
祭神は、天道日女命・御炊屋姫命だが、天道日女命一座の説もあり<ref>式内社の研究 第一巻</ref>。
[[國津比古命神社]]と、対面している。地元では、両社を合わせて「風早宮大氏神(かざはやみやのおおうじがみ)」と呼ぶ。
== 境内社 == *素戔嗚神社(祭神:[[素戔嗚命]])
*和霊神社(祭神:[[山家公頼]])は、別法人(参考:[[和霊神社]])
== 例祭 == [[体育の日]]の前々日に行われる。「宵の明星」と呼ばれる。
== 脚注 ==
{{reflist}}{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたまひめのみことしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧郷社]] [[Category:松山市の建築物]] [[Category:松山市の歴史]] [[Category:伊予国|社0]]

{{Shrine |Name=Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine |Image=[[File:Kushitamahimenomikoto-jinja 20240523 (3).jpg|260px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|15|N|132|47|49.5|E}}}} |Location={{flag|Ehime Prefecture}} [[Matsuyama, Ehime|Matsuyama]] Takata 702-1 |Latitude=33 |Longitude=132 |LatDegrees=33|LatMinutes=58|LatSeconds=15 |LonDegrees=132|LonMinutes=47|LonSeconds=49.5 |Deities={{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |MainHall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |AnnualFestival=Day before the day before Sports Day |Map=Japan Ehime }}
'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Matsuyama, Ehime|Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社}} and formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
The small hill in front of the [[haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient burial mound known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Tumulus, which spans 75 meters and features a [[zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命}}, enshrining {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinai Shrines, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社}}).
== Annual Festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kazahaya District, Iyo|ja|風早郡}}
== References ==

{{Shrine |Name=Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine |Image=[[File:Kushitamahimenomikoto-jinja 20240523 (3).jpg|260px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|15|N|132|47|49.5|E}}}} |Location={{flag|Ehime Prefecture}} [[Matsuyama, Ehime|Matsuyama]] Takata 702-1 |Latitude=33 |Longitude=132 |LatDegrees=33|LatMinutes=58|LatSeconds=15 |LonDegrees=132|LonMinutes=47|LonSeconds=49.5 |Deities={{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |MainHall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |AnnualFestival=Day before the day before Sports Day |Map=Japan Ehime }}

The small hill in front of the [[haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient burial mound known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Tumulus, which spans 75 meters and features a [[zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命}}, enshrining {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinai Shrines, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社}}).

The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[Sports Day]].

{{Shrine |Name=Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine |Image=[[File:Kushitamahimenomikoto-jinja 20240523 (3).jpg|260px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|15|N|132|47|49.5|E}}}} |Location={{flag|Ehime Prefecture}} [[Matsuyama, Ehime|Matsuyama]] Takata 702-1 |Latitude=33 |Longitude=132 |LatDegrees=33|LatMinutes=58|LatSeconds=15 |LonDegrees=132|LonMinutes=47|LonSeconds=49.5 |Deities={{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |MainHall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |AnnualFestival=Day before the day before Sports Day |Map=Japan Ehime }}
'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Matsuyama, Ehime|Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社}} and formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
The small hill in front of the [[haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient burial mound known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Tumulus, which spans 75 meters and features a [[zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinai Shrines, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社}}).
== Annual Festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kazahaya District, Iyo|ja|風早郡}}

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Matsuyama, Ehime|Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社}} and formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
The small hill in front of the [[haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient burial mound known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Tumulus, which spans 75 meters and features a [[zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinai Shrines, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[Sports Day]].

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Matsuyama, Ehime|Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社}} and formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
The small hill in front of the [[haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinai Shrines, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kazahaya District, Iyo|ja|風早郡}}

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Matsuyama, Ehime|Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社}} and formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
The small hill in front of the [[haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinai Shrines, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[Sports Day]].

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=櫛玉比賣命神社 |画像 = [[画像:Kushitamahimenomikoto-jinja 20240523 (3).jpg|260px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|15|N|132|47|49.5|E}}}} |所在地=愛媛県松山市高田甲702-1 | 緯度度 = 33| 緯度分 = 58| 緯度秒 = 15 | 経度度 = 132| 経度分 = 47| 経度秒 = 49.5 |祭神=[[天道日女命]]・[[御炊屋姫命]] |社格=式内小社、旧[[郷社]] |本殿 = [[流造]] |例祭=体育の日の前々日 |地図 = Japan Ehime }}
'''櫛玉比賣命神社'''(くしたまひめのみことじんじゃ)は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]高田に鎮座する[[式内社]]。旧社格は、[[郷社]]。
[[拝殿]]の前方の小山は、[[前方後円墳]]の一部分で、全長が75mの櫛玉比賣命神社古墳と呼ばれる。
== 概要 == [[応神天皇]]の代に[[勅令]]を奉じ、物部阿佐理命が早風の[[国造]]となり、[[天道日女命]]及び、[[御炊屋姫命]]を奉斎<ref>つつしみ祀ること</ref>した。
祭神は、天道日女命・御炊屋姫命だが、天道日女命一座の説もある<ref>式内社の研究 第一巻</ref>。
[[國津比古命神社]]と、対面している。地元では、両社を合わせて「風早宮大氏神(かざはやみやのおおうじがみ)」と呼ぶ。
== 境内社 == *素戔嗚神社(祭神:[[素戔嗚命]])
*和霊神社(祭神:[[山家公頼]])は、別法人(参考:[[和霊神社]])
== 例祭 == [[体育の日]]の前々日に行われる。「宵の明星」と呼ばれる。
== 関連項目 == *[[伊予国の式内社一覧]] *[[風早郡]]
== 脚注 ==
{{reflist}}{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたまひめのみことしんしや}} [[Category:松山市の神社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧郷社]] [[Category:松山市の建築物]] [[Category:松山市の歴史]] [[Category:伊予国|社0]]

'''櫛玉比賣命神社'''(くしたまひめのみことじんじゃ)は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]高田に鎮座する[[式内社]]。旧社格は、[[郷社]]。
[[拝殿]]の前方の小山は、[[前方後円墳]]の一部分で、全長が75mの櫛玉比賣命神社古墳と呼ばれる。
== 概要 == [[応神天皇]]の代に[[勅令]]を奉じ、物部阿佐理命が早風の[[国造]]となり、[[天道日女命]]及び、[[御炊屋姫命]]を奉斎<ref>つつしみ祀ること</ref>した。
祭神は、天道日女命・御炊屋姫命だが、天道日女命一座の説もある<ref>式内社の研究 第一巻</ref>。
[[國津比古命神社]]と、対面している。地元では、両社を合わせて「風早宮大氏神(かざはやみやのおおうじがみ)」と呼ぶ。

*和霊神社(祭神:[[山家公頼]])は、別法人(参考:[[和霊神社]])

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kushitamahimenomikoto-jinja 20240523 (3).jpg|260px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|15|N|132|47|49.5|E}}}} |location={{flag|Ehime Prefecture}} [[Matsuyama, Ehime|Matsuyama]] Takata 702-1 |Latitude=33 |Longitude=132 |LatDegrees=33|LatMinutes=58|LatSeconds=15 |LonDegrees=132|LonMinutes=47|LonSeconds=49.5 |deity={{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}} |MainHall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |AnnualFestival=Day before the day before Sports Day |Map=Japan Ehime }}
'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Matsuyama, Ehime|Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}}.
The small hill in front of the [[haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}}, the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|qq=draft}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinai Shrines, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kazahaya District, Iyo|ja|風早郡|qq=draft}}

The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinai Shrines, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft}}).

The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[Sports Day]].

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kushitamahimenomikoto-jinja 20240523 (3).jpg|260px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|15|N|132|47|49.5|E}}}} |location={{flag|Ehime Prefecture}} [[Matsuyama, Ehime|Matsuyama]] Takata 702-1 |Latitude=33 |Longitude=132 |LatDegrees=33|LatMinutes=58|LatSeconds=15 |LonDegrees=132|LonMinutes=47|LonSeconds=49.5 |deity={{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |MainHall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |AnnualFestival=Day before the day before Sports Day |Map=Japan Ehime }}
'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Matsuyama, Ehime|Matsuyama]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}.
The small hill in front of the [[haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|qq=}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|qq=}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|qq=}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|qq=}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[Sports Day]].

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Matsuyama, Ehime|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.
The small hill in front of the [[haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>

{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|qq=}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|qq=}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[Sports Day]].

* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|qq=}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).

The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[Sports Day]].

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|qq=}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|qq=}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[Sports Day]].

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kushitamahimenomikoto-jinja 20240523 (3).jpg|260px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|15|N|132|47|49.5|E}}}} |location={{flag|Ehime Prefecture}} [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]] Takata 702-1 |Latitude=33 |Longitude=132 |LatDegrees=33|LatMinutes=58|LatSeconds=15 |LonDegrees=132|LonMinutes=47|LonSeconds=49.5 |deity={{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |MainHall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |AnnualFestival=Day before the day before Sports Day |Map=Japan Ehime }}
'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|qq=}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|qq=}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kushitamahimenomikoto-jinja 20240523 (3).jpg|260px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|15|N|132|47|49.5|E}}}} |location={{flag|Ehime Prefecture}} [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]] Takata 702-1 |Latitude=33 |Longitude=132 |LatDegrees=33|LatMinutes=58|LatSeconds=15 |LonDegrees=132|LonMinutes=47|LonSeconds=49.5 |deity={{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |MainHall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |AnnualFestival=Day before the day before Sports Day |Map=Japan Ehime }}
'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|qq=}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|qq=}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|qq=}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|qq=}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|qq=|1=Kunitsuhiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|qq=|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kushitamahimenomikoto-jinja 20240523 (3).jpg|260px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|15|N|132|47|49.5|E}}}} |location={{flag|Ehime Prefecture}} [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]] Takata 702-1 |Latitude=33 |Longitude=132 |LatDegrees=33|LatMinutes=58|LatSeconds=15 |LonDegrees=132|LonMinutes=47|LonSeconds=49.5 |deity={{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |MainHall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |AnnualFestival=Day before the day before Sports Day |Map=Japan Ehime }}
'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|qq=|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|qq=|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|qq=|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|qq=|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|qq=|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|qq=|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|qq=|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|qq=|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|qq=|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|qq=|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|qq=|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫛玉比賣命神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kushitamahimenomikoto-jinja 20240523 (3).jpg|260px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|15|N|132|47|49.5|E}}}} |location={{flag|Ehime Prefecture}} [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]] Takata 702-1 |Latitude=33 |Longitude=132 |LatDegrees=33|LatMinutes=58|LatSeconds=15 |LonDegrees=132|LonMinutes=47|LonSeconds=49.5 |deity={{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |MainHall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |AnnualFestival=Day before the day before Sports Day |Map=Japan Ehime }}
'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫛玉比賣命神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kushitamahimenomikoto-jinja 20240523 (3).jpg|260px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|15|N|132|47|49.5|E}}}} |location={{flag|Ehime Prefecture}} [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]] Takata 702-1 |Latitude=33 |Longitude=132 |LatDegrees=33|LatMinutes=58|LatSeconds=15 |LonDegrees=132|LonMinutes=47|LonSeconds=49.5 |deity={{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |MainHall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |AnnualFestival=Day before the day before Sports Day |Map=Japan Ehime }}
'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).

== Overview ==

During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>

The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>

{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).

== Auxiliary Shrines ==

* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}.
* **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).

== festival ==

The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
