<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫛玉比賣命神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kushitamahimenomikoto-jinja 20240523 (3).jpg|260px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|15|N|132|47|49.5|E}}}} |location={{flag|Ehime Prefecture}} [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]] Takata 702-1 |Latitude=33 |Longitude=132 |LatDegrees=33|LatMinutes=58|LatSeconds=15 |LonDegrees=132|LonMinutes=47|LonSeconds=49.5 |deity={{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |MainHall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |AnnualFestival=Day before the day before Sports Day |Map=Japan Ehime }}
'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:櫛玉比賣命神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kushitamahimenomikoto-jinja 20240523 (3).jpg|260px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|15|N|132|47|49.5|E}}}} |location={{flag|Ehime Prefecture}} [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]] Takata 702-1 |Latitude=33 |Longitude=132 |LatDegrees=33|LatMinutes=58|LatSeconds=15 |LonDegrees=132|LonMinutes=47|LonSeconds=49.5 |deity={{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |MainHall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |AnnualFestival=Day before the day before Sports Day |Map=Japan Ehime }}
'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]
== References ==

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the [[:en:Haiden|Haiden]] (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * [[:en:Kazahaya District, Ehime|Kazahaya District, Iyo]]

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the {{ill|Haiden|en|Haiden|WD=Q1569100|lt=Haiden}} (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the {{ill|Haiden|en|Haiden|WD=Q1569100|lt=Haiden}} (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kazahaya District, Ehime|en|Kazahaya District, Ehime|WD=Q6380771|lt=Kazahaya District, Iyo}}

This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kazahaya District, Ehime|en|Kazahaya District, Ehime|WD=Q6380771|lt=Kazahaya District, Iyo}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kushitamahimenomikoto-jinja 20240523 (3).jpg|260px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|15|N|132|47|49.5|E}}}} |location={{flag|Ehime Prefecture}} {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama}} Takata 702-1 |Latitude=33 |Longitude=132 |LatDegrees=33|LatMinutes=58|LatSeconds=15 |LonDegrees=132|LonMinutes=47|LonSeconds=49.5 |deity={{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |MainHall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |AnnualFestival=Day before the day before Sports Day |Map=Japan Ehime }}
'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the {{ill|Haiden|en|Haiden|WD=Q1569100|lt=Haiden}} (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kazahaya District, Ehime|en|Kazahaya District, Ehime|WD=Q6380771|lt=Kazahaya District, Iyo}}

{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kazahaya District, Ehime|en|Kazahaya District, Ehime|WD=Q6380771|lt=Kazahaya District, Iyo}}

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the {{ill|Haiden|en|Haiden|WD=Q1569100|lt=Haiden}} (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kazahaya District, Ehime|en|Kazahaya District, Ehime|WD=Q6380771|lt=Kazahaya District, Iyo}}

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the {{ill|Haiden|en|Haiden|WD=Q1569100|lt=Haiden}} (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kazahaya District, Ehime|en|Kazahaya District, Ehime|WD=Q6380771|lt=Kazahaya District, Iyo}}

'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the {{ill|Haiden|en|Haiden|WD=Q1569100|lt=Haiden|1=Haiden}} (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kazahaya District, Ehime|en|Kazahaya District, Ehime|WD=Q6380771|lt=Kazahaya District, Iyo|1=Kazahaya District, Ehime}}

* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kazahaya District, Ehime|en|Kazahaya District, Ehime|qid=Q6380771|lt=Kazahaya District, Iyo|1=Kazahaya District, Ehime}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kushitamahimenomikoto-jinja 20240523 (3).jpg|260px]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|33|58|15|N|132|47|49.5|E}}}} |location={{flag|Ehime Prefecture}} {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}} Takata 702-1 |Latitude=33 |Longitude=132 |LatDegrees=33|LatMinutes=58|LatSeconds=15 |LonDegrees=132|LonMinutes=47|LonSeconds=49.5 |deity={{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11064932}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |MainHall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |AnnualFestival=Day before the day before Sports Day |Map=Japan Ehime }}
'''Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine''' (櫛玉比賣命神社, Kushitamahimenomikoto Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the {{ill|Haiden|en|Haiden|qid=Q1569100|lt=Haiden|1=Haiden}} (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kazahaya District, Ehime|en|Kazahaya District, Ehime|qid=Q6380771|lt=Kazahaya District, Iyo|1=Kazahaya District, Ehime}}

It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Sho-sha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Sho-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11064932}} and formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}.
The small hill in front of the {{ill|Haiden|en|Haiden|qid=Q1569100|lt=Haiden|1=Haiden}} (worship hall) is part of an ancient Kofun known as the Kushitamahimenomikoto Shrine Kofun, which spans 75 meters and features a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
== Overview ==
During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, the shrine was established under an imperial edict, with the local ruler, {{ill|Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja|物部阿佐理命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Asari-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}.<ref>"Reverently enshrining the deities."</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11467754}}. This shrine is under a separate administration (see {{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warei Shrine|qq=}}).
== festival ==
The shrine’s annual festival, called "Evening Star" (宵の明星), takes place on the day before the day before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]].
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|ja|伊予国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kazahaya District, Ehime|en|Kazahaya District, Ehime|qid=Q6380771|lt=Kazahaya District, Iyo|1=Kazahaya District, Ehime}}

The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohohi-no-Mikoto|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q121355874}} and {{ill|Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja|御炊屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikiyanohime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, though some traditions claim the enshrinement of Amenohohi-no-Mikoto alone.<ref>Research on Shikinaisha, Volume 1.</ref>
{{ill|Kunitsuhiko Shrine|ja|國津比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsuhiko Shrine|1=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q126709405}} stands opposite this shrine, and both shrines are collectively referred to locally as "Kazahaya-miya Ōujigami" (風早宮大氏神).
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* **Susanoo Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. * **Warei Shrine**: Dedicated to {{ill|Yamanouchi Kōrai|ja|山家公頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanouchi Kōrai|1=Yanbe Kinmochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11467754}}.
